description: Consolidate UX improvements across all three actor workflows: (1) End user: opentabs start auto-initializes config + extension, npm plugins auto-discovered from global node_modules, remove redundant CLI commands. (2) Plugin developer: opentabs-plugin build auto-registers in config localPlugins and calls POST /reload. (3) Platform developer: simplify publish script to use single npm token. Also: ungate POST /reload, split config into plugins (auto-discovered) vs localPlugins (explicit), update side panel to show local/npm distinction, fix misleading messages.

userStories.title: Add localPlugins field to config and update config parsing
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want config.json to distinguish localPlugins from auto-discovered npm plugins so that the system knows which plugins are local dev projects.
acceptanceCriteria: OpentabsConfig interface has localPlugins: string[] field
parseConfigRecord extracts localPlugins from raw config (defaults to [] if missing)
The existing plugins field is removed from OpentabsConfig
Migration: if raw config has a plugins array, entries that look like local paths (start with ./ or / or ~/) are moved to localPlugins, npm package names are dropped (they will be auto-discovered)
loadConfig auto-creates config with { localPlugins: [], tools: {}, secret: <uuid> }
Config file at ~/.opentabs/config.json uses localPlugins instead of plugins
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts. Replace plugins with localPlugins in OpentabsConfig. Update parseConfigRecord to extract localPlugins. Add migration: if raw config has 'plugins' array, filter local paths into localPlugins and log a notice about npm plugins being auto-discovered now. Update the default config template. Update all consumers of config.plugins to use config.localPlugins.

userStories.title: Add npm global package auto-discovery to the resolver
userStories.description: As an end user, I want the MCP server to auto-discover globally installed opentabs-plugin-* packages so that I don't need to manually register npm plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: New function discoverGlobalNpmPlugins() in resolver.ts returns an array of resolved plugin directories
It finds the global node_modules path via npm root -g (cached, called once at startup)
It scans for directories matching opentabs-plugin-* and @*/opentabs-plugin-* patterns
It returns the absolute path to each matching directory that contains a valid package.json with an opentabs field
Scoped packages like @org/opentabs-plugin-foo are discovered
The scan is a single readdir + filter, not recursive
Errors during scan are collected, not thrown
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/mcp-server/src/resolver.ts. Add discoverGlobalNpmPlugins(). Use Bun.spawnSync(['npm', 'root', '-g']) to get the global path (cache it in a module-level variable). Then readdir the path, filter for opentabs-plugin-* entries. For scoped packages, readdir each @scope/ directory. For each match, check that package.json exists and has an opentabs field (quick validation). Return array of absolute directory paths. Also check bun's global path via Bun.spawnSync(['bun', 'pm', '-g', 'bin']) and derive node_modules from it, in case the user installed with bun add -g.

userStories.title: Update discovery to merge npm auto-discovered + local plugins
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the discovery orchestrator to combine auto-discovered npm plugins with explicit local plugins so that both sources are unified in one registry.
acceptanceCriteria: discoverPlugins takes localPlugins (string[]) and configDir as input
It calls discoverGlobalNpmPlugins() to find npm plugins automatically
It resolves localPlugins paths via resolvePluginPath
Local plugins override npm plugins of the same name (local wins)
Trust tier: npm auto-discovered = 'community' or 'official' (based on scope), local = 'local'
The DiscoveryResult includes a source field per plugin: 'npm' or 'local'
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.ts. Change the input from specifiers: string[] to localPlugins: string[]. Call discoverGlobalNpmPlugins() for npm plugins. Resolve local plugins via resolvePluginPath. Load both sets. When merging, if same plugin name exists in both local and npm, keep the local version. Build the registry from the merged set.

userStories.title: Update reload.ts to pass localPlugins to discovery
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want reloadCore to pass config.localPlugins to the discovery orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: reloadCore calls discoverPlugins(config.localPlugins, configDir) instead of discoverPlugins(config.plugins, configDir)
All references to config.plugins in reload.ts are updated to config.localPlugins
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts. Change line ~165 from config.plugins to config.localPlugins. Search for any other references to config.plugins in the file and update.

userStories.title: Ungate POST /reload endpoint
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want POST /reload to be always available so that my build tooling can trigger server rediscovery.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /reload works in both dev and production modes
The isDev() check and 405 response are removed from the /reload handler
Bearer auth is still required
Rate limiting is still in place
The /health endpoint still reports mode as dev or production
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts. Remove the isDev() check at lines 208-213. The endpoint remains behind bearer auth and rate limiting. Update the comment above the handler to remove 'dev mode only'.

userStories.title: opentabs-plugin build auto-registers in localPlugins and calls /reload
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want opentabs-plugin build to auto-register my plugin in config and notify the server so that my plugin is immediately available for testing.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful build, opentabs-plugin build reads ~/.opentabs/config.json
If the current plugin directory is not already in localPlugins, it adds it (as path relative to config dir)
Config is written atomically (same pattern as scaffold.ts autoRegisterPlugin)
After registration, opentabs-plugin build calls POST http://localhost:9515/reload with bearer auth from config secret
If the server is not running, the /reload call fails silently (build still succeeds)
If config.json does not exist, registration is skipped with a warning
In --watch mode, /reload is called after each rebuild (not just the first)
Registration only happens once (first build), reload happens every build
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts. After the build completes (after generating tools.json), add two new steps: (1) readAndRegisterInConfig() — reads ~/.opentabs/config.json, checks if CWD is in localPlugins, adds if not, writes atomically. Use homedir() + '.opentabs/config.json' to find the config. (2) notifyServer() — reads secret from config, POST to http://localhost:<port>/reload with Authorization: Bearer <secret>. Port defaults to 9515 (or read from OPENTABS_PORT env). Both steps are wrapped in try/catch — failures are warnings, not errors. The build itself must succeed even if the server isn't running.

userStories.title: opentabs start auto-initializes config and extension
userStories.description: As an end user, I want opentabs start to auto-create config and install the extension on first run so that I don't need separate setup commands.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs start creates ~/.opentabs/ directory if it does not exist
opentabs start creates ~/.opentabs/config.json with defaults if it does not exist
opentabs start installs the extension to ~/.opentabs/extension/ if it does not exist or if the version has changed
The auto-install uses the same logic as the current opentabs setup command
On first run, opentabs start prints first-time setup instructions after the server starts
Setup instructions include: how to load extension in Chrome, how to configure MCP client
On subsequent runs, setup instructions are not repeated (check for extension already loaded or a marker file)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts. Before spawning the server process, call the extension install logic (from setup.ts). Check if ~/.opentabs/extension/manifest.json exists — if not, install. Check .opentabs-version marker — if version changed, reinstall. For the config, the MCP server's loadConfig() already auto-creates it, so we don't need to duplicate that in the CLI. But we should ensure ~/.opentabs/ directory exists before spawning the server. After the server starts (stdout shows 'listening on'), print setup instructions if this is a first-time run.

userStories.title: Remove redundant CLI commands: config init, setup, plugin add, plugin remove, plugin list
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want to remove redundant CLI commands so that the CLI is focused and clean.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs config init command is removed (auto-init is in opentabs start)
opentabs setup command is removed (auto-install is in opentabs start)
opentabs plugin add command is removed (npm plugins auto-discovered, local plugins auto-registered by build)
opentabs plugin remove command is removed (npm uninstall removes the package, local plugins removed by deleting from config)
opentabs plugin list command is removed (opentabs status shows plugins)
The corresponding source files are deleted or the functions removed
commands/index.ts no longer exports removed command registrations
cli.ts no longer registers removed commands
opentabs --help shows: start, status, doctor, logs, plugin create, config show/set/path
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/cli.ts — remove registrations. Delete platform/cli/src/commands/setup.ts (logic moved to start.ts in US-007). Remove config init from platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts. Remove plugin add, plugin remove, plugin list from platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts — keep only plugin create. The plugin.ts file should be simplified to only register the create subcommand. Delete any unused imports and helper functions. Keep the handlePluginAdd logic available as a shared utility if start.ts or build.ts needs it.

acceptanceCriteria: opentabs status plugin list shows each plugin with a source label: (npm) or (local)
The /health endpoint includes a source field per plugin: 'npm' or 'local'
opentabs status --json includes the source field
Failed plugins are shown with their error message

The source can be derived from trustTier ('local' → 'local', anything else → 'npm') or from the DiscoveryResult source field added in US-003. Edit platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts to display the source label.

The badge is subtle (small text or icon) and does not clutter the UI

Keep the badge minimal — perhaps a small outlined tag next to the plugin name.

userStories.title: Remove scaffold auto-registration from config (moved to build)

acceptanceCriteria: scaffoldPlugin no longer calls autoRegisterPlugin
autoRegisterPlugin function is removed from scaffold.ts (or moved to a shared utility if needed by build.ts)
The 'Registered in ~/.opentabs/config.json' output line is removed
The 'Next steps' output still shows: cd, bun install, bun run build

bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts. Remove the try/catch block that calls autoRegisterPlugin (lines 413-420). The autoRegisterPlugin function can be deleted entirely — the build tool (US-006) has its own registration logic. Update the console output to remove the 'Registered' line.

userStories.title: Simplify publish script to use single npm token
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the publish script to use the ~/.npmrc token directly so that I don't need a separate publish token file.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/publish.sh no longer references ~/.npmrc.publish
scripts/publish.sh no longer swaps/backs up/restores ~/.npmrc
scripts/publish.sh no longer has a trap/cleanup handler for token restoration
scripts/publish.sh verifies npm whoami succeeds before publishing
The setup instructions in the script header are simplified to just: ensure ~/.npmrc has a token with read+write access
bun run build passes (publish script is not type-checked but should be syntactically valid)
userStories.notes: Edit scripts/publish.sh. Remove the PUBLISH_NPMRC variable, the existence check for ~/.npmrc.publish, the cp/backup/restore logic, and the trap handler. Replace with a simple npm whoami check at the start to verify auth is working. The header comment should be updated to explain the simplified setup: one token in ~/.npmrc with read+write access.

userStories.title: Update E2E tests for new config format and discovery
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests to work with the new localPlugins config and npm auto-discovery.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E fixtures create config with localPlugins instead of plugins
E2E tests that test plugin discovery work with localPlugins
Hot reload tests that modify config use localPlugins field
Tests that add/remove plugins from config use localPlugins
The createMinimalPlugin helper in fixtures.ts uses localPlugins
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: Edit e2e/fixtures.ts and all E2E test files. Search for 'plugins' in config creation/modification and change to 'localPlugins'. The E2E test plugins are local filesystem plugins registered by path, so they should all go in localPlugins. npm auto-discovery won't affect E2E tests since they use isolated config directories.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md for new UX design
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want CLAUDE.md to accurately document the new plugin discovery, CLI commands, and config format.
acceptanceCriteria: Config section shows localPlugins field instead of plugins
Plugin discovery section explains: npm auto-discovery from global node_modules + localPlugins from config
CLI commands section lists: start, status, doctor, logs, plugin create, config show/set/path
Removed commands (config init, setup, plugin add/remove/list) are not mentioned
Plugin development workflow explains: build auto-registers and calls /reload
Publishing section explains simplified single-token setup
No references to old plugins array or npmPlugins in config
userStories.notes: Edit CLAUDE.md. Update the config file section, CLI commands section, plugin discovery section, development workflow section, and publishing section. Remove references to opentabs setup, config init, plugin add/remove.
